prompt_rules: single large village house object designed as one coherent building, not repeated wall/roof stamps
SFC-style pixel-art house that may later be sliced into 16x16 engine tiles
break the rectangular box silhouette with roof overhangs, chimney, uneven eaves, trim, windows, door detail, and grounding shadow
use varied roof and wall texture, subtle dirt, shadow, and small props while keeping a readable collision footprint

prompt:
A single large wooden TORII GATE as one coherent pixel-art object for a bright mythical-Japan action RPG field, front view. Vermilion-lacquered round pillars with a gentle weathered texture, a dark tiled top lintel with slightly upturned ends, a thick braided sacred rope (shimenawa) with white zigzag paper streamers (shide) hanging between the pillars, small stone bases, a soft contact shadow on the ground. Bright, welcoming, sacred but cheerful. The gate is drawn LARGE filling most of the canvas with a small margin. NO characters, no text. Crisp high-resolution 16-bit-style pixel art, fine readable pixel clusters, dark one-pixel outline, top-left light.
Avoid: no Mario/Nintendo/Zelda/Final Fantasy/Pokemon/Dragon Quest/Secret of Mana/Okami/Sakuna characters, logos or likeness, no samurai armor, no kabuto helmet, no ninja outfit, no two-sword daisho, no gun, no modern objects, no horror mood, no dark gloomy palette, no bird or companion creature in the sheet, no letter emblems, no modern text/watermark/UI.
Output: crisp high-resolution 16-bit-style pixel art with fine readable pixel clusters, dark one-pixel outline, top-left light source, bright cheerful colors, solid #FF00FF magenta background filling all empty space, consistent identity and scale across frames.
